title: josephia tridentata
intro: Josephia tridentata, also known as Bigelow sage or Mountain big sagebrush, is a shrub native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Josephia tridentata include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for use in traditional medicine.
